coverage.destination: Dworshak Reservoir / Orofino / Ahsahka corridor, Idaho
notes: Second-pass enrichment: more corridor anchors, live-check templates, and planner humor rows—still not a substitute for Idaho 511, USACE, or reservation systems.
First-pass but broad trip dataset for the Billy's Big Adventure planner UI.

Narrative voice intentionally blends Pee-wee-scale quest energy with Hangover-style wolfpack logistics humor (PG-13). Movie nods are for morale, not a playbook—obey laws, signs, MVUM/OHV rules, and Idaho 511.
Practical records (alerts, closures, reservations) stay factual; fun_option rows are explicitly silly.
Availability, prices, hours, closures, and road conditions change fast; re-check live sources before departure.

narrative_voice.disclaimer: No endorsement of impaired driving, wildlife harassment, or Vegas-grade mistakes—Idaho edition runs on snacks, sleep, and spreadsheets.

source_registry.title: Dworshak Dam closes some recreation areas for public safety

route_profiles.name: Fastest practical corridor
route_profiles.summary: Use Coeur d'Alene to Lewiston corridor on US-95, then continue toward Orofino and the Dworshak/Ahsahka area. Coeur d'Alene to Orofino is about 135.2 road miles and about 3h 1m before adding the last stretch to Big Eddy / Ahsahka.
notes: Exact turn-by-turn path is not stored here because apps can map that live.
Big Eddy is described by USACE as 5 miles west of Orofino on Highway 7, then 3 miles to Big Eddy.
Always re-check Idaho 511 before departure.
Wolfpack logistics: one navigator, one 511 checker, nobody ‘remembers’ the whole corridor from group chat.

route_profiles.name: History-heavy side quest chain
route_profiles.summary: If you want the route itself to become part of the trip, chain together Confluence Overlook, Nez Perce NHP Visitor Center, and Hells Gate State Park before continuing east toward Orofino.
notes: This is not the shortest way to get there, but it adds actual substance instead of random filler stops.
Pee-wee would call this ‘the scenic director’s cut’; still pack water and patience.

route_profiles.name: Extend eastward after Orofino
route_profiles.summary: If Day 2 needs more to do, keep going east from Orofino toward Weippe and the historic Lolo Trail lane.
notes: Day-2 extension if the wolfpack still has daylight and nobody lost the keys in Orofino.

route_profiles.name: Lewiston / Clarkston services pivot
route_profiles.summary: If you need a full-size hospital backup, big-box restock, or a longer meal break before the grade, the Lewiston–Clarkston metro is the natural corridor hub between Coeur d'Alene and Orofino.
notes: Adds time and complexity; use when the wolfpack actually needs services, not when everyone is hangry five minutes from departure.
Crossing into Washington for fuel or food is fine—just remember which state’s hands-free / traffic laws you are in.

route_profiles.name: Live maps compare (no static turn-by-turn)
route_profiles.summary: Rome2Rio gives a distance/time sanity check; Google Maps (or Apple Maps) should own the actual turn-by-turn on trip day because construction and detours move faster than JSON.
notes: If US-95 near Orofino is ugly, compare an alternate suggested by the live map engine—but still verify against Idaho 511.

alerts.name: Recent Dworshak recreation closures
alerts.summary: USACE said Merry's Bay Day Use Area, Dent Acres Recreation Area, and Bruce's Eddy Recreation Area are temporarily closed for public safety due to flooding and mudslides. Dam View Campground, Canyon Creek Recreation Area, and Big Eddy Recreation Area remain open. Dent Campground and Dent Group Camping Area were scheduled to open April 12.
alerts.action: Do not build the trip around Dent or Merry's Bay for an April 6 outing unless live status changes.

alerts.name: Clearwater County flooding / mudslide context
alerts.summary: The Orofino / Clearwater County area has been dealing with flooding and mudslide impacts in March 2026. Use Idaho 511 and local emergency channels before leaving.
notes: This record intentionally avoids hard-coding transient lane details because those can change faster than a static file.

alerts.name: Official events snapshot for Apr 6, 2026
alerts.summary: Clearwater County Chamber calendar shows no events scheduled specifically for April 6, 2026.

alerts.name: Weather + forest alert preflight
alerts.summary: Early April can still throw snow, ice, wind, and lowland rain mixes on the grade and reservoir roads. Forest closures and incident traffic also happen—check NWS and Nez Perce-Clearwater alerts alongside Idaho 511.
alerts.action: Night before and morning of: skim forecasts for Coeur d'Alene, Lewiston, and Orofino; scan forest alerts if you intend any dirt-road side quests.

alerts.name: Fishing license + boat inspection awareness
alerts.summary: If the crew will actually fish or launch a motorized boat, Idaho Fish and Game license rules and invasive species / inspection expectations apply. Rules and station hours are not static in JSON.
alerts.action: Assign one person to confirm licenses and any required inspections before you pack rods and hulls.

alerts.name: Weak cell service around reservoir shorelines
alerts.summary: Dworshak State Park documentation calls out unreliable cell service in-park; similar dead pockets are common along big western reservoirs. Plan meetup times and offline maps accordingly.
alerts.action: Download offline maps, agree on rally points, and do not assume group chat works at the boat ramp.

alerts.name: Emergency kit / household preparedness (Idaho OEM angle)
alerts.summary: Long rural drives benefit from basics: water, warm layers, flashlight, first aid, and a plan if someone needs urgent care. Idaho OEM publishes general preparedness guidance.

entities.name: Confluence Overlook
entities.summary: Highway pullout overlooking the confluence of the Snake and Clearwater Rivers, with views across the Nez Perce homeland and a historical marker.

location.description: about 8 miles north of Lewiston on the Lewiston Grade of US-95

entities.name: Nez Perce National Historical Park Visitor Center
entities.summary: Substantial visitor center, museum, and historic district stop that adds real depth to the route instead of disposable roadside filler.

hours.note: Check NPS for current hours and programming.

entities.name: Hells Gate State Park + Lewis & Clark Discovery Center
entities.summary: Big, serviceable stop near Lewiston with discovery center, marina, camping, cabins, bike access, and a strong 'gateway adventure' feel if the upper route gets messy.

hours.visitor_center: Open daily 9 AM–5 PM; closed major holidays

notes: Useful as a fallback overnight or rain-plan stop.

entities.name: Winchester Lake State Park
entities.summary: A quieter detour south of Lewiston if the trip grows into a multi-stop Idaho wander instead of a straight Dworshak punch-in.

entities.name: Orofino downtown / business district
entities.summary: Main practical basecamp zone for food, rooms, pharmacy, groceries, theater, and a quick reset before pushing out toward the reservoir.

entities.name: Clearwater Historical Museum
entities.summary: Local history museum focused on Clearwater River Country, including Nez Perce history, Lewis and Clark, mining, homesteading, farming, and logging.

hours.seasonal: Open Saturdays after Memorial Day until Labor Day 10 AM–2 PM

entities.name: Rex Theater
entities.summary: Historic Orofino movie house and cultural landmark if the birthday trip wants a goofy old-school evening layer.

entities.name: Dworshak National Fish Hatchery
entities.summary: Major hatchery at the confluence of the North Fork and main Clearwater River, 3 miles west of Orofino.

location.description: 3 miles west of Orofino on the Clearwater / North Fork confluence

entities.name: Dworshak Visitor Center
entities.summary: Free visitor center with exhibits, maps, videos, and a small gift shop. Good weekday add-on if you want more than just shoreline time.

hours.summer: Open 7 days a week from Memorial Day weekend through Labor Day weekend

rules: Pets not allowed in visitor center

entities.name: Dworshak State Park
entities.summary: Primary park anchor on the western shore of Dworshak Reservoir with campsites, cabins, group camp options, boat access, rentals, fishing support, and weak cell service.
constraints: Cell service is unreliable within the park
Wi-Fi is only noted at the Big Eddy Marina conference center

entities.name: Freeman Creek Cabins
entities.summary: Four park cabins sleeping five each with electricity, heat, and air conditioning.

location.address: Dworshak State Park / Freeman Creek area

entities.name: Three Meadows Group Camp
entities.summary: Large group lodging and camp zone with cabins, lodge, kitchen, fire pit, courts, amphitheater, and private boat dock.
notes: Probably overkill for a tiny birthday crew, but useful if this idea snowballs.

entities.name: Big Eddy Recreation Area + Marina
entities.summary: Open now, close to Orofino, year-round general access, and one of the safest bets for a first Dworshak run during current closure conditions.

location.description: 5 miles west of Orofino on Highway 7, then follow signs for 3 miles to Big Eddy

entities.name: Canyon Creek Recreation Area
entities.summary: USACE says Canyon Creek remains open. Good backup if Big Eddy is busy or you want a second zone to explore.

entities.name: Dent Acres / Dent Campground
entities.summary: Temporarily closed in the March 2026 public-safety announcement. Dent Campground and group camping were scheduled to open April 12, which is after the birthday date.

entities.name: Merry's Bay Day Use Area
entities.summary: Temporarily closed in the March 2026 USACE safety update due to flooding / mudslides.

entities.name: Weippe Discovery Center
entities.summary: Small interpretive stop for the Lewis and Clark / Nez Perce meeting story if you push east beyond Orofino.

entities.name: Weippe Prairie
entities.summary: Publicly accessible historic landscape where Lewis & Clark first met the Nez Perce, with sweeping prairie views.

entities.name: Tubbs Hill (Coeur d'Alene)
entities.summary: Popular in-town hike above Lake Coeur d'Alene—good leg-stretch before a long US-95 run if the crew is already in CDA.

location.description: Trailheads near downtown Coeur d'Alene; check local maps for parking

notes: Not on the direct line to Dworshak—use when origin day starts in CDA with spare morning time.

entities.name: McEuen Park + downtown lakefront
entities.summary: Central lakefront park space in Coeur d'Alene for a calm stroll, kid energy burn, or last downtown coffee before wheels-up.

entities.name: Lewiston–Clarkston riverfront trails
entities.summary: Snake / Clearwater confluence towns have extensive levee and riverfront trail mileage—useful low-cost break if you pivot through Lewiston for services.

entities.name: Dworshak Dam public-facing overview
entities.summary: USACE hub page for the project—useful orientation before visitor center hours, tours, or closure rules; pair with alerts for what is actually open.

entities.name: Big Eddy camping / overnight options (verify live)
entities.summary: Big Eddy is a primary day-use and marina anchor; if the wolfpack wants to sleep nearer the water than Orofino motels, check USACE and reservation systems for what camping inventory exists and is open under current closures.

location.description: Follow USACE directions to Big Eddy Recreation Area

notes: Treat as a planning flag, not a guarantee—USACE pages and alerts change with water and safety work.

entities.name: Helgeson Place Hotel & Suites
entities.summary: Historic 20-suite hotel in Orofino with full kitchens and downtown access.

entities.name: White Pine Motel
entities.summary: 18-room downtown-adjacent motel with walking access to local amenities, Wi-Fi, and pet-friendly positioning.

entities.name: Konkolville Motel
entities.summary: Motel about three miles from downtown Orofino, set in a canyon near Orofino Creek.

entities.name: Best Western Lodge at River's Edge
entities.summary: Comfort-upgrade option on Main Street with pool / hot tub / breakfast type amenities and direct booking support.

entities.name: Hayes Foods and Hardware
entities.summary: Useful one-stop practical stop for grocery and hardware-type needs before heading farther into the reservoir corridor.

entities.name: Atkinson fuel network (Orofino)
entities.summary: Orofino fuel network including main office cardlock / credit card pumps and Deano's convenience store pumps.

entities.name: Deano's / Exxon Orofino
entities.summary: Fuel / convenience option on Highway 12 with long daily hours and diesel.

entities.name: Lewiston–Clarkston big-box / grocery corridor
entities.summary: If you forgot cooler ice, camp fuel, or trail snacks, the LC Valley has the usual national and regional retail density—trade a little time on US-95 for selection.
notes: No single address here on purpose; use maps search near your chosen exit.

entities.name: Arnzen's Glenwood Pharmacy
entities.summary: Local full-service pharmacy in Orofino.

entities.name: Clearwater Canyon Pharmacy
entities.summary: Additional pharmacy option in Orofino.

entities.name: Orofino Municipal Airport fuel
entities.summary: Probably not core to this trip, but the municipal airport notes 24/7 fuel purchase via credit card.

entities.name: Dining on the Edge
entities.summary: More special-occasion dinner play with river views.

entities.name: Pizza Factory Orofino
entities.summary: Simple group-friendly pizza option in town.

entities.name: Ponderosa Restaurant and Brass Rail Lounge
entities.summary: Family-style restaurant open long hours, useful when timing matters more than aesthetics.

entities.name: Lewiston–Clarkston casual dining (live pick)
entities.summary: Chamber/tourism listings cover a wide spread of cafes, pubs, and family spots on both sides of the river—better discovered live than frozen in JSON.

entities.name: Deano's Restaurant (Orofino)
entities.summary: Grill / restaurant tied to the Deano's travel-center footprint on Highway 12—useful when the group wants food near the same stop as fuel.

entities.name: Surron / motorized trail caution
entities.summary: Idaho Parks allows Class 1 and 2 e-bikes in state parks and on department-administered trails, but Idaho requires OHV numbering for motorbikes / dirtbikes / ATVs / UTVs. Nez Perce-Clearwater MVUMs are the official maps for motorized travel decisions.
notes: Whether a specific Surron setup qualifies as an e-bike or should be treated as a motorbike depends on the actual configuration and where you ride.
Do not assume state-park shoreline paths or random forest roads are legal without checking the applicable map/rules.

entities.name: Visitor center roadway ATV note
entities.summary: The Dworshak Visitor Center notes that ATVs licensed as a motor vehicle are allowed on the roadways in that area.

entities.name: Boat ramp usability context
entities.summary: The official Dworshak map lists usable elevation ranges for major ramps. Big Eddy has the deepest usable range in the map table, which helps explain why it stays valuable when conditions are sketchy elsewhere.

entities.name: Invasive species / watercraft inspection expectations
entities.summary: Idaho runs a watercraft inspection program aimed at preventing aquatic invasive species. Where, when, and whether your hull must stop depends on route, season, and current station status.
notes: Pair with fishing license checks if the trip is half birthday party, half fish heist.

entities.name: Wildlife on highway corridor (dawn/dusk vigilance)
entities.summary: North Idaho highways routinely cross deer and elk habitat. April is not a free pass—keep speeds sane, use high beams when appropriate, and expect animals on shoulders near fields and timber.

entities.name: Clearwater Valley Health emergency / clinic support
entities.summary: Primary medical support in Orofino; emergency department is always open.

entities.name: Clearwater County Sheriff
entities.summary: County sheriff and backcountry medic request point if the trip goes sideways in remote terrain.

entities.name: Clearwater County Emergency Management
entities.summary: Good emergency-management contact to know when the area is in active flood / slide conditions.

entities.name: St. Joseph Regional Medical Center
entities.summary: Regional full-service hospital backup in Lewiston if something bigger than local urgent needs comes up.

entities.name: AAA Idaho / roadside assistance (membership product)
entities.summary: If someone in the wolfpack already carries AAA, keep the app and card in the real world—not buried in a glove box cosplay bit.
notes: Not a substitute for 911; membership terms and coverage vary by level.

entities.name: Personal vehicle baseline
entities.summary: Using Idaho regular gas average and the Coeur d'Alene-to-Orofino drive distance as a baseline, fuel remains the cleanest budget play if you already have a vehicle that can haul your gear.
entities.formula_note: Fuel cost = round-trip miles / mpg * gas price

entities.name: U-Haul cargo van baseline
entities.summary: U-Haul cargo van has two seats and about 246 cu ft of cargo space. Good for hauling, not magically good for comfort.

entities.name: Home Depot cargo van concept
entities.summary: Home Depot advertises cargo vans starting at $19 and says its moving vehicles have unlimited mileage, but local branch availability needs to be checked directly.

entities.name: Lodging logic
entities.summary: For a small crew, the usual money order is: own vehicle + tent if you already own gear, then own vehicle + budget motel, then van/truck only if bike hauling solves a real problem, then comfort hotel / cabin if the crew can split enough to flatten the hit.

entities.name: Licenses, inspections, and small permit math
entities.summary: Fishing licenses and some boating-related fees are per-person or per-season numbers that change with rule updates. Model them as a line item the navigator updates the week of the trip.

entities.name: Snack + ice floor (group sanity)
entities.summary: Even a tight birthday trip benefits from a deliberate per-person snack + hydration floor so the wolfpack does not cannibalize gas-station roller food as a meal plan.

entities.name: Airbnb search
entities.summary: Use live booking search for vacation rentals because inventory and price change too fast for static JSON.
notes: App should treat this as an external launch, not static inventory.

entities.name: VRBO search
entities.summary: Backup vacation-rental search template.

entities.name: Idaho 511
entities.summary: Mandatory last-minute road check source.

entities.name: Idaho Parks reservations
entities.summary: Use for Dworshak campsite / cabin checks.

entities.name: Motor Vehicle Use Maps
entities.summary: App should link users to the official motorized-travel maps before presenting any Surron route ideas.

entities.name: Booking.com hotel search (Orofino)
entities.summary: Alternate hotel aggregator launch point; rates and inventory change constantly.

entities.name: NWS forecast (search Orofino)
entities.summary: Launch a weather.gov search for destination-town conditions; pair with WFO Spokane hub for regional context.

entities.name: Clearwater County events calendar
entities.summary: Browse beyond the single April 6 snapshot alert when you want festivals, races, or town closures.

entities.name: Idaho Fish and Game license purchase
entities.summary: Buy or verify licenses before you pack rods.

entities.name: Museum-grade birthday convoy
entities.summary: Pee-wee energy: treat the convoy as a traveling exhibit. Assign one friend to narrate every gas stop like a solemn docent while the wolfpack tries not to laugh.

entities.name: Velvet-rope moon suite
entities.summary: Hangover-adjacent luxury cosplay: battery candles, handwritten room-service cards, one unserious concierge with a clipboard. Actual sleep quality not guaranteed.

entities.name: Rooftop headcount (Idaho edition)
entities.summary: Before leaving any lot, one dramatic roll call like you misplaced a groomsman. You are beside a Subaru and a guilt cooler—not a Vegas roof. Still: count everyone.

entities.name: Something in the cooler
entities.summary: Movie lore, snack reality: one sticker-bombed or padlocked cooler that is 90% sandwiches and 10% myth. Assign a cooler historian to log each opening.

entities.name: The Alamo stop — reservoir cut
entities.summary: One non-negotiable photo stop nobody fully explains but everyone obeys—a sign, a rock, a fish statue. Pee-wee rules; wolfpack attendance mandatory.

entities.name: Adult “next morning” kit
entities.summary: Water, electrolytes, real breakfast, sunglasses, dignity. The movie had chaos; your trip has ibuprofen only if your doctor says OK—and zero mystery pills.

entities.name: One-verse singalong tax
entities.summary: Pick one ridiculous song. Exactly one verse at the fuel pump. Whoever knows all the words navigates the next leg—fair tax, cruel jury.

entities.name: Tuxedo tow package
entities.summary: Black-tie meets muddy ramps: everyone dresses gala-ready while unloading bikes like valet chaos broke the timeline. The wolfpack pretends this was planned.

entities.name: Ceremonial goose escort (fiction only)
entities.summary: Data-only joke: seven imaginary geese ‘bless’ the convoy. Real geese are wildlife—observe from distance; do not harass. Pee-wee would use puppets; you use restraint.

entities.name: Birthday manifest clipboard
entities.summary: One laminated checklist titled ‘Official Birthday Expedition’ with boxes for snacks, chargers, and emotional support cooler. Crossing items off is mandatory theater.

entities.name: Dam selfie lighting czar
entities.summary: Assign one friend to care deeply about golden-hour angles at the dam while everyone else pretends this is Cannes. Keep tripods off roadways and respect closures.

entities.name: Wolfpack treasurer (non-Vegas edition)
entities.summary: One person holds the shared fuel + ice spreadsheet and actually sends Venmo requests before anyone ‘forgets’ at the pump. Boring beats chaos.

entities.name: Motel room number cipher
entities.summary: Text the room number to the group chat using an unnecessarily elaborate cipher. Then immediately send the plaintext because adults have luggage and zero patience.

entities.name: Reservoir echo roll call
entities.summary: At a safe, legal pullout, one very short celebratory shout tests canyon acoustics—then immediate indoor voices because other visitors did not opt into your bit.

road_and_weather_checks: Re-check Dworshak USACE closure page for reservoir access status.
If planning anything motorized off normal roads, confirm legal travel using the correct MVUM / local rules.
Skim NWS Spokane (WFO OTX) headlines for northern Idaho / eastern WA if snow, wind, or river fog could affect the grade.
If launching a boat, scan Idaho invasive species / inspection pages the same week as departure.
recommended_app_behaviors: Treat alerts and booking search templates as dynamic/high-priority records.
Show closed / mixed-status destination entities before open fun stops.
If user toggles Surron mode, surface legal caution records before ride ideas.
If user toggles budget mode, sort by budget_score descending and practical_score descending.
When listing corridor POIs, prefer grouping by zone (CDA → Lewiston → Orofino → reservoir) to match how people actually drive the day.
Surface communications_dead_zone style alerts whenever the user selects camping or marina-heavy entities.
known_gaps: No live Airbnb/VRBO inventory is embedded because rental supply changes too fast.
No static list of every gas station or restaurant on the corridor is embedded; this file focuses on useful anchors rather than exhaustive map spam.
Road lane control details around Orofino are intentionally abstracted to Idaho 511 because those can change faster than a saved file.
Lewiston–Clarkston dining and big-box stops are pointed at tourism/search surfaces rather than frozen addresses.
Second-pass rows add breadth, not real-time road geometry—live maps still win for detours.
wolfpack_reminders: Headcount before major moves—movies skip it; your trip should not.
If the crew splits attention, assign a navigator and a separate 511 checker.
fun_option rows are jokes; alerts and search_template rows are the serious channel.
Hydration and sleep are part of logistics, not a punchline—plan both.
Screenshot or print critical reservation codes; Big Eddy and state-park pockets may not load your email.
Decide in advance who owns fishing-license and boat-inspection homework so it is not a parking-lot argument.
Keep one paper Idaho / reservoir map in the glove box as backup when the group chat goes silent.
